hypothesis: Combining the Ichimoku cloud to confirm price is in a clear trend direction with an ADX filter for trend strength reduces false breakouts; ATR-based risk management adapts to market volatility, limiting drawdowns while capturing genuine trend moves.

A breakout above or below the Ichimoku cloud signals a strong directional move; confirming it with a high ADX (>25) filters out weak trends, while ATR-based stops and targets control volatility‑driven risk, leading to higher win rates in a 30‑minute market.

The Ichimoku cloud provides a dynamic support/resistance framework; breaking its upper/lower band signals a strong directional move while an ADX above 25 confirms trend strength, reducing whipsaw entries in sideways markets. ATR‑based risk limits adapt to volatility, tightening stops in calm periods and scaling TP in turbulent times to improve risk‑reward.

Using the Ichimoku cloud as a dynamic support/resistance provides a clear breakout signal; filtering with ADX > 25 confirms a strong trend, while ATR-based stop/TP adapts to volatility, limiting losses in choppy markets and capturing moves in trending periods.

A breakout above or below the Ichimoku cloud signals a shift in market momentum; filtering with ADX > 25 confirms a strong trend, reducing whipsaw entries, while ATR‑based risk limits adapt to volatility, improving Sharpe and curbing drawdown.

A breakout above or below the Ichimoku cloud indicates a strong directional move; filtering with ADX > 25 ensures the trend is sustained, while ATR-based stop and take‑profit levels adapt to market volatility and limit drawdown.
A breakout only when the price crosses the Ichimoku cloud and the ADX is not only above 25 but also increasing, ensuring a strong, expanding trend; ATR-based exits limit exposure to volatile swings.

A breakout above the Ichimoku upper cloud confirms a bullish momentum that is further validated by a rising ADX above 25, indicating a strong trend; the ATR-based stop and TP capture volatility-adjusted risk and reward, while exiting when the price re-enters the cloud or the ADX weakens reduces exposure to false breakouts.
